description_long: This is a laboratory-style bottle with a round, bulbous base and a long, narrow neck, reminiscent of a Florence flask. The main body is smooth and white, with a matte finish that suggests it is made of ceramic or opaque glass. The neck is topped with a brownish cap that appears to be made of wood or cork, providing a secure closure. The bottle's surface is free of any visible labels, branding, or decorative elements, giving it a utilitarian appearance. There are no visible signs of damage, scratches, or deformation on the exterior. The bottle is likely used for storing liquids or chemicals, but its opaque body prevents any view of the interior contents.
description: A smooth, white, round-bottomed bottle with a long neck and a brown cap, resembling a laboratory flask. The surface is matte and unadorned, with no visible labels or damage.
description_view_0: Front view shows the full round base and the long neck with the brown cap clearly visible.
description_view_1: Side view is nearly identical to the front, with the same round base and neck proportions visible.
description_view_2: Back view mirrors the front, with no additional features or markings visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the same smooth, round base and long neck with the cap.
